title: Hello World
description:
Print exactly one line: Hello, World!

**Input Format:** None

**Output Format:** A single line containing exactly: Hello, World!

**Constraints:** None

title: Sum of Two Numbers
description:
Read two integers from stdin and output their sum.

**Input Format:** Two lines, each containing one integer.

**Output Format:** A single integer - the sum.

**Constraints:** -10^9 â‰¤ a, b â‰¤ 10^9

title: Maximum in Array
description:
Given an array of n integers, find and print the maximum element.

**Input Format:** First line: n (size). Second line: n space-separated integers.

**Output Format:** A single integer - the maximum value.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5, -10^9 â‰¤ elements â‰¤ 10^9

title: Reverse a String
description:
Given a string s, reverse it and output the result.

**Input Format:** A single line containing the string s.

**Output Format:** The reversed string.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ |s| â‰¤ 1000

title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of n integers and a target, find two indices (0-based) whose values add up to target. Assume exactly one solution exists.

**Input Format:** First line: n and target (space-separated). Second line: n space-separated integers.

**Output Format:** Two space-separated integers - the indices.

**Constraints:** 2 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^4, -10^9 â‰¤ target, elements â‰¤ 10^9

title: Factorial (Recursion)
description:
Compute n! (n factorial) using recursion. 0! = 1.

**Input Format:** A single integer n.

**Output Format:** n!

**Constraints:** 0 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 15

title: Bubble Sort Count
description:
Given an array, count the number of swaps needed to sort it using bubble sort.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n space-separated integers.

**Output Format:** Number of swaps.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 100

title: Binary Search
description:
Given a sorted array and target, return the 0-based index of target, or -1 if not found.

**Input Format:** First line: n and target. Second line: n sorted integers.

**Output Format:** Index or -1.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5

title: Valid Palindrome
description:
Given a string, determine if it is a palindrome (reads same forwards and backwards). Ignore non-alphanumeric chars and case.

**Input Format:** A single line string.

**Output Format:** YES or NO.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ |s| â‰¤ 2Ã—10^5
sample_input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

test_cases.input_data: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

title: Max Sum Subarray of Size K
description:
Given an array and integer k, find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size k.

**Input Format:** First line: n and k. Second line: n integers.

**Output Format:** Maximum sum.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ k â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5

title: Valid Parentheses
description:
Given a string containing only '(', ')', '{', '}', '[', ']', determine if the brackets are balanced.

**Input Format:** A single line string.

**Output Format:** YES or NO.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ |s| â‰¤ 10^4

title: Climbing Stairs
description:
You climb stairs. Each step you can take 1 or 2 steps. How many distinct ways to reach the top (n steps)?

**Input Format:** A single integer n.

**Output Format:** Number of distinct ways.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 45

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description:
Given array of daily stock prices, find max profit from one buy and one sell. If no profit possible, output 0.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n integers (prices).

**Output Format:** Maximum profit.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5, 0 â‰¤ price â‰¤ 10^4

title: Count Set Bits
description:
Given a positive integer n, count the number of 1s in its binary representation.

**Input Format:** A single integer n.

**Output Format:** Number of set bits.

**Constraints:** 0 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^9

title: FizzBuzz
description:
Given an integer n, for each integer i from 1 to n inclusive, print one of the following: if i is divisible by 3 and 5 print "FizzBuzz"; if divisible by 3 print "Fizz"; if divisible by 5 print "Buzz"; otherwise print i. Output one value per line.

**Input Format:** A single integer n.

**Output Format:** n lines - each line is "Fizz", "Buzz", "FizzBuzz", or the number i.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 100
sample_output:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz
7
8
Fizz
Buzz
11
Fizz
13
14
FizzBuzz

test_cases.expected_output:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz
7
8
Fizz
Buzz
11
Fizz
13
14
FizzBuzz

title: Contains Duplicate
description:
Given an array of integers, output YES if any value appears at least twice, else NO.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n integers.

**Output Format:** YES or NO.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5

title: Move Zeros
description:
Given an array, move all zeros to the end while preserving the relative order of non-zero elements.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n integers.

**Output Format:** Space-separated integers (modified array).

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating
description:
Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. A substring is a contiguous non-empty sequence of characters within a string. For example, for "abcabcbb" the longest substring without repeating characters is "abc" with length 3.

**Input Format:** A single line containing the string s.

**Output Format:** A single integer - the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

**Constraints:** 0 â‰¤ |s| â‰¤ 5Ã—10^4, s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces

title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
description:
Given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, sorted in non-decreasing order, merge them into a single sorted array. The merged array should be sorted in non-decreasing order. You may assume both input arrays are already sorted.

**Input Format:** First line: n m (sizes of the two arrays). Second line: n space-separated integers (first array). Third line: m space-separated integers (second array).

**Output Format:** Space-separated merged sorted array.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n, m â‰¤ 10^5, -10^9 â‰¤ elements â‰¤ 10^9

title: Group Anagrams
description:
Given n strings, group them by anagrams. Output each group on a new line as space-separated words. Order of groups doesn't matter.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Next n lines: strings.

**Output Format:** Each group on one line, words space-separated.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^4, 1 â‰¤ |each| â‰¤ 100
sample_input:
6
eat
tea
tan
ate
nat
bat
sample_output:
eat tea ate
bat
tan nat

test_cases.input_data:
6
eat
tea
tan
ate
nat
bat
test_cases.expected_output:
eat tea ate
bat
tan nat

title: Fibonacci (Memoization)
description:
Compute the nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed). F(0)=0, F(1)=1, F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2).

**Input Format:** A single integer n.

**Output Format:** F(n).

**Constraints:** 0 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 40

title: Kth Largest in Array
description:
Given an array and k, find the kth largest element (1-indexed, so 1st largest is max).

**Input Format:** First line: n and k. Second line: n integers.

**Output Format:** The kth largest element.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ k â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description:
There is an integer array nums sorted in ascending order that has been rotated at an unknown pivot. For example, [0,1,2,4,5,6,7] might become [4,5,6,7,0,1,2]. Given the array and a target value, return the 0-based index of target if it is in the array, or -1 if it is not.

**Input Format:** First line: n and target (space-separated). Second line: n integers representing the rotated sorted array.

**Output Format:** A single integer - the 0-based index of target, or -1 if not found.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 5000, -10^4 â‰¤ nums[i], target â‰¤ 10^4

title: Container With Most Water
description:
Given n vertical lines (heights), find two lines that form a container with maximum area.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n integers (heights).

**Output Format:** Maximum area.

**Constraints:** 2 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5, 0 â‰¤ height â‰¤ 10^4

title: Minimize Maximum Subarray Sum
description:
You are given an array of integers and an integer k. Split the array into k non-empty contiguous subarrays. The score of a split is the largest sum of any subarray. Return the minimum possible score. For example, splitting [7,2,5,10,8] into 2 subarrays can give [7,2,5] and [10,8] with max sum 18.

**Input Format:** First line: n and k (space-separated). Second line: n non-negative integers.

**Output Format:** A single integer - the minimum possible maximum subarray sum.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ k â‰¤ n â‰¤ 1000, 0 â‰¤ nums[i] â‰¤ 10^6

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description:
You are given an array of strings tokens that represents a valid arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation (postfix notation). Valid operators are +, -, *, and /. Each operand may be an integer or another expression. The division between two integers truncates toward zero. Evaluate the expression and return the result. For example, ["2","1","+","3","*"] evaluates to ((2 + 1) * 3) = 9.

**Input Format:** A single line of space-separated tokens (integers or operators +, -, *, /).

**Output Format:** A single integer - the result of the expression.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ tokens â‰¤ 10^4, -200 â‰¤ token value â‰¤ 200

title: Coin Change
description:
Given coins denominations and amount, return minimum number of coins to make amount. If impossible, -1.

**Input Format:** First line: n (coin count) and amount. Second line: n coin values.

**Output Format:** Minimum coins or -1.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 12, 0 â‰¤ amount â‰¤ 10^4

title: Jump Game
description:
Array represents max jump from each index. Can you reach the last index? Output YES or NO.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n non-negative integers.

**Output Format:** YES or NO.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^4

title: Power of Two
description:
Given integer n, output YES if n is a power of 2, else NO.

**Input Format:** A single integer n.

**Output Format:** YES or NO.

**Constraints:** -2^31 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 2^31 - 1

title: GCD of Two Numbers
description:
Compute the greatest common divisor of two positive integers using Euclidean algorithm.

**Input Format:** Two space-separated integers a and b.

**Output Format:** GCD(a, b).

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ a, b â‰¤ 10^9

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description:
Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring in s. A palindrome reads the same forward and backward. For example, "babad" has palindromic substrings "bab" and "aba"; the longest is "bab". If multiple solutions exist, output any valid one.

**Input Format:** A single line containing the string s.

**Output Format:** The longest palindromic substring. If multiple exist, any valid one.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ |s| â‰¤ 1000, s consists only of printable ASCII characters

title: Implement Queue using Stacks
description:
Implement a FIFO queue using only two stacks. Support push, pop, peek, empty.

**Input Format:** First line: number of operations. Then operations: PUSH x, POP, PEEK, EMPTY.

**Output Format:** For POP/PEEK output value. For EMPTY output true/false.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ ops â‰¤ 100

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description:
Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence. A subsequence is derived by deleting some or no elements without changing the order of the remaining elements. For example, [3,6,2,7] has LIS length 3 ([3,6,7] or [2,7]).

**Input Format:** First line: n (array size). Second line: n space-separated integers.

**Output Format:** A single integer - the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 2500, -10^4 â‰¤ nums[i] â‰¤ 10^4

title: Single Number
description:
Array has one element that appears once; every other appears twice. Find the single one.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n integers.

**Output Format:** The single number.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 3Ã—10^4

title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
description:
Given a 1-indexed array of integers that is already sorted in non-decreasing order, find two numbers such that they add up to a specific target. Return the 1-indexed indices of the two numbers. You may assume that each input has exactly one solution and you may not use the same element twice.

**Input Format:** First line: n (array size) and target. Second line: n space-separated sorted integers.

**Output Format:** Two space-separated integers (1-indexed indices).

**Constraints:** 2 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 3Ã—10^4, -1000 â‰¤ numbers[i] â‰¤ 1000, -1000 â‰¤ target â‰¤ 1000

title: LRU Cache
description:
Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache. Implement get(key) and put(key, value). get returns the value if key exists, else -1. put updates the value if key exists, otherwise inserts. When capacity is exceeded, invalidate the least recently used item.

**Input Format:** First line: capacity. Then each line: GET k or PUT k v (k=key, v=value).

**Output Format:** For each GET, output the value or -1 on a new line.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ capacity â‰¤ 3000, 0 â‰¤ key, value â‰¤ 10^4, 1 â‰¤ operations â‰¤ 10^5
sample_input:
2
PUT 1 1
PUT 2 2
GET 1
PUT 3 3
GET 2
PUT 4 4
GET 1
GET 3
GET 4

test_cases.input_data:
2
PUT 1 1
PUT 2 2
GET 1
PUT 3 3
GET 2
PUT 4 4
GET 1
GET 3
GET 4

3
PUT 1 1
PUT 2 2
PUT 3 3
GET 1
PUT 4 4
GET 2

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
description:
Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays. The overall run time complexity should be O(log(m+n)).

**Input Format:** First line: m n (sizes). Second line: m integers (nums1). Third line: n integers (nums2).

**Output Format:** The median as a decimal (e.g. 2.0 or 2.5). Print exactly one decimal place.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ m, n â‰¤ 1000, -10^6 â‰¤ nums[i] â‰¤ 10^6

title: Merge K Sorted Arrays
description:
Given k sorted arrays, merge them into one sorted array. This is a classic problem asked at Google and Meta to test understanding of heap/merge techniques.

**Input Format:** First line: k (number of arrays). Then k lines: each line has n_i followed by n_i sorted integers.

**Output Format:** Space-separated merged sorted array.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ k â‰¤ 100, 0 â‰¤ total elements â‰¤ 10^4

title: Word Ladder
description:
Given a begin word, end word, and a word list, find the length of the shortest transformation sequence from begin to end such that only one letter can be changed at a time and each transformed word must exist in the word list. Return 0 if no such sequence exists.

**Input Format:** First line: beginWord. Second line: endWord. Third line: n (word list size). Next n lines: words in the list.

**Output Format:** Length of shortest transformation (including begin and end). Output 0 if impossible.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ |word| â‰¤ 10, beginWord â‰  endWord, 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 5000

cog

title: Course Schedule
description:
There are numCourses courses labeled 0 to numCourses-1. Some courses have prerequisites. Given the total number of courses and a list of prerequisite pairs [a,b] meaning you must take b before a, determine if you can finish all courses.

**Input Format:** First line: numCourses and number of prerequisite pairs. Each following line: a b (take b before a).

**Output Format:** YES if you can finish all courses, NO otherwise.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ numCourses â‰¤ 2000, 0 â‰¤ prerequisites â‰¤ 5000

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description:
You are given an array of integers nums and an integer k. There is a sliding window of size k that moves from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position, return the maximum element in the current window. For nums = [1,3,-1,-3,5,3,6,7] and k = 3, the result is [3,3,5,5,6,7].

**Input Format:** First line: n and k (space-separated). Second line: n space-separated integers.

**Output Format:** Space-separated maximum values for each window position.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ k â‰¤ n â‰¤ 10^5, -10^4 â‰¤ nums[i] â‰¤ 10^4

title: Minimum Window Substring
description:
Given two strings s and t, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window. If no such substring exists, return the empty string.

**Input Format:** First line: string s. Second line: string t.

**Output Format:** The minimum window substring. If multiple exist, any valid one.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ |s|, |t| â‰¤ 10^5, s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters

title: Trapping Rain Water
description:
Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n integers (heights).

**Output Format:** Total amount of rainwater that can be trapped.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 2Ã—10^5, 0 â‰¤ height[i] â‰¤ 10^5

title: 3Sum
description:
Given an integer array, find all unique triplets that sum to zero. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets. Output each triplet as three space-separated numbers in ascending order, one triplet per line. Triplets can be in any order.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Second line: n integers.

**Output Format:** Each line: three space-separated integers (a b c with a â‰¤ b â‰¤ c). Output nothing if no triplets.

**Constraints:** 3 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 3000, -10^5 â‰¤ nums[i] â‰¤ 10^5

title: N-Queens
description:
Place n queens on an nÃ—n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Output all distinct solutions.

**Input Format:** A single integer n.

**Output Format:** For each solution, output n lines where each line has n characters: '.' for empty, 'Q' for queen. Separate solutions with a blank line.

**Constraints:** 1 â‰¤ n â‰¤ 9

title: Longest Valid Parentheses
description:
Given a string containing only '(' and ')', find the length of the longest valid (well-formed) parentheses substring.

**Input Format:** A single string containing only '(' and ')'.

**Output Format:** The length of the longest valid parentheses substring.

**Constraints:** 0 â‰¤ |s| â‰¤ 3Ã—10^4

title: Number of Islands
description:
Given a 2D grid of 1 (land) and 0 (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.

**Input Format:** First line: rows cols. Next rows lines: space-separated 0s and 1s.

**Output Format:** Number of islands.

**Constraints:** 1 ≤ rows, cols ≤ 300

title: Keys and Rooms
description:
There are n rooms labeled 0 to n-1. Each room has some keys. You start in room 0. Return YES if you can visit every room, NO otherwise.

**Input Format:** First line: n. Next n lines: each line has k then k key numbers (room indices you can go to).

**Output Format:** YES or NO.

**Constraints:** 2 ≤ n ≤ 1000

title: Find the Town Judge
description:
In a town of n people labeled 1 to n, there is a town judge. The judge trusts nobody; everybody trusts the judge. Given trust array [a,b] meaning a trusts b, find the judge. Return -1 if no judge.

**Input Format:** First line: n and number of trust pairs. Next lines: a b (a trusts b).

**Output Format:** The judge label or -1.

**Constraints:** 1 ≤ n ≤ 1000
